output.headerText:
foundational-dsa — a trilingual reference implementing 41 data structures & algorithms in Java (canonical), Python, and JavaScript, with 1:1 parity across all three (123 = 41x3) plus per-item docs.

Layout: java/ (Maven + JUnit 5, com.dsa.examples) is the canonical reference; python/ (pytest, dsa package) and javascript/ (Jest, src/ + test/) are ports; docs/ holds API.md (cross-language canonical API) and per-item READMEs; tools/ holds the parity/matrix/stub checks and test runners.

Reading guide: every algorithm exists in all three languages with mirrored names; tools/parity.mjs and tools/matrix.mjs enforce that coverage, so to understand one item compare its Java/Python/JS files side by side. Complexity and invariants live in each implementation's module doc-block and in docs/API.md.
